channel.title: NYT > World > Asia Pacific
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How Japan Reacted to Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke
item.description: Some people criticized President Trump’s decision to invoke a painful chapter of history. Others worried it might harm U.S.-Japan relations.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump met with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Using Charm and Restraint, Japan’s Leader Mostly Avoids Trump’s Wrath
item.description: During her first visit to the White House, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi drew praise from President Trump. But the war in the Middle East will test their relationship.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ms. Takaichi and President Trump in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Iran War Fallout: Southeast Asia Hard Hit by Skyrocketing Fuel Prices
item.description: Across Southeast Asia, a region heavily dependent on energy exports brought via the Strait of Hormuz, lives are being upended by higher oil and gas prices.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Jason Gutierrez, Muktita Suhartono, Kittiphum Sringammuang and Tung Ngo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Lauren DeCicca for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fishermen on their boats while docked and unable to go out to sea due to diesel shortages and high prices, in Samut Sakhon, Thailand, on Monday.

item.title: How the Iran War Narrowed Flight Corridors Between Europe and Asia
item.description: One of the few paths left between the two continents threads through Azerbaijan, Georgia and Turkey, as global conflicts complicate aviation logistics.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Planes parked at Dubai International Airport on Wednesday, as airspace closures across the Middle East disrupted flight routes.

item.title: Scramble for Jet Fuel Shows How Energy Shortages Are Rippling Across Asia
item.description: As China and other major suppliers of refined oil in the region restrict exports, import-dependent countries are desperate to secure fuel supplies.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson, Aaron Krolik, Tung Ngo and Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Wellington International Airport in New Zealand. Air New Zealand has canceled about 1,100 flights through early May.

item.title: Trump’s Complaint About Israeli Strike on Gas Field Exposes Divergent Strategies
item.description: President Trump said he told Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel he disapproved of the attack, which sent energy markets reeling. But Israeli officials said the Americans were informed beforehand.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump has been trying to preserve Iran’s oil and gas infrastructure and keep the country from retaliating at energy facilities throughout the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Trump Jokes About Pearl Harbor in Meeting With Japan’s Prime Minister
item.description: Breaking a taboo, President Trump needled Japan’s prime minister about the World War II attack, as she widened her eyes and appeared to take a deep breath in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

item.title: Attacks on Oil and Natural Gas Facilities Could Lead to Much Higher Prices
item.description: Attacks on oil and natural gas facilities this week could make it much harder for Persian Gulf countries to rebuild and restart production when the war eventually ends.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rose from an oil site in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, on Saturday. Energy experts say more attacks could cause prices to surge.

item.title: How Japan’s Leader Sanae Takaichi Found Her Voice in D.C. Decades Ago
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi got her start in politics as an intern for a trailblazing Colorado lawmaker. She returns to Washington this week to meet with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno

item.title: Gas Prices in Japan Hit Record High, Testing Leader’s Cost-of-Living Pledge
item.description: Japan is deploying subsidies to blunt fuel price increases but remains vulnerable to the long-term economic risks of the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The average retail price of gasoline in Japan climbed to 191 yen, or about $1.20, per liter on Monday.

item.title: Ozempic Is About to Go Generic in India, China and Canada
item.description: In India, China and several other nations, Novo Nordisk is on the verge of losing patent protection for its blockbuster weight loss drug, opening the door for cheaper competing versions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins, Meaghan Tobin, Eshe Nelson, Alex Travelli and Pragati K.B.
item.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times

item.title: Pakistan Pauses Afghanistan Airstrikes for Eid
item.description: At least 143 people were killed in a Pakistani airstrike that hit a drug rehabilitation facility in Kabul on Monday, according to a top U.N. official.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Safiullah Padshah and Zia ur-Rehman
item.media:description: A mass burial was held on Wednesday for people who died on Monday in a Pakistani airstrike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul, Afghanistan.

item.title: Trump’s Friendship With Japan’s Leader Takaichi Faces Test Over Iran
item.description: In Washington on Thursday, President Trump is expected to press Sanae Takaichi for military help in the Strait of Hormuz. But she faces constraints on what she can offer.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan aboard the U.S.S. George Washington at Yokosuka Port south of Yokohama, Japan, in October.

item.title: Asia Turns Back to Coal as War Chokes Off Natural Gas
item.description: Across Asia, a sharp drop in liquefied natural gas supplies is pushing major importers back toward coal, undermining L.N.G.’s long-held role as a stable energy anchor.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Qatar Energy announced a shutdown of its liquefied natural gas production after Iranian attacks on energy installations.

item.title: China Ramps Up Scrutiny of Meta’s Acquisition of Manus
item.description: The country appears to be cracking down on people linked to the acquisition of Manus, a Singapore company with Chinese roots, as President Trump prepares to visit Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson, Meaghan Tobin, Paul Mozur and Eli Tan
item.media:description: Meta announced in December that it had bought Manus, a Singapore artificial intelligence start-up that has Chinese roots.

item.title: China Is Embracing OpenClaw, a New A.I. Agent, and the Government Is Wary
item.description: Excitement about A.I. assistant tools is running into growing concerns about the security risks of software that operates autonomously on user’s devices.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Xinyun Wu
item.media:description: People sought help from engineers to install OpenClaw, an open-source A.I. assistant, in China last week.

item.title: Dozens Dead in Pakistani Airstrike on Kabul
item.description: The attack hit a drug rehabilitation facility, and Afghanistan vowed to retaliate, risking an escalation of the conflict between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Safiullah Padshah and Elian Peltier
item.media:description: The airstrike hit a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul, Afghan officials said.

item.title: What the Iran War Means for the U.S.-China Relationship
item.description: With the Xi-Trump summit almost certainly delayed, and tensions rising over the war in Iran, vital issues for both the U.S. and China are also being cast into uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Chinese destroyer Tangshan at dock near an Iranian navy ship in Cape Town, South Africa, in January for joint maritime safety operations.

item.title: Trump Officials Look to More Managed Approach to Trade With China
item.description: The administration has begun discussing a new “Board of Trade” with China to try to balance what U.S. officials see as an unequal economic relationship.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: Scott Bessent, the Treasury secretary, left, and Jamieson Greer, the U.S. trade representative, conducted talks with their Chinese counterparts on Sunday and Monday in Paris.

item.title: Trump’s Threat to Delay Summit With Xi Casts New Shadow Over China Relations
item.description: President Trump warned that he could postpone a meeting set to begin in just over two weeks if China refuses to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, China’s leader, at their last in-person meeting in Busan, South Korea, in October.
